summary: Good sound quality captures an energetic MSG opener with blazing Rain → Shakedown and China > I Know You Rider sequences, though the second half loses steam.
review: The recording (source type unknown) is clear enough to enjoy the high‑energy first set, highlighted by the powerful Rain→Shakedown opening and a tight China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition. Reviewers rave about Jerry’s funky tone and Vince’s keyboard work, but several note that the show drops off after the early highlights, making for an uneven overall experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the musical performance and specific song highlights while noting a decline in later material; no concrete recording‑quality comments but sound is presumed adequate.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No explicit comments on soundboard vs. audience or clarity; reviewers focus on performance, implying the audio is at least listenable.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Estimated Prophet > Eyes of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong opening combo (Rain→Shakedown→Wang Dang→Cold Rain) and solid second‑set China>Rider segment, but the show loses momentum after the early high points.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for funky tone on Shakedown, washboard strumming in China>Rider, and passionate vocals on Cold Rain.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince highlighted as being in rare form, delivering superb keyboard work throughout the set.
